body.notes: | BLOCKED: Encode async-by-design semantic in primitives catalog before implementing. prim_mcp_send must be non-blocking: push result to TWM, not blocking step runner. | Revisit after T-habit-templates — MCP call may be expressible as a template pattern.
body.title: MCP primitives: mcp_send + mcp_await for habit-to-tool message passing
body.result: MCPCALL instruction added to node_executor.py (MEDIUM inertia, additive only). Dispatches to tool registry synchronously v1. Handles unknown tools + exceptions gracefully. 4 new tests. 1655 total pass. Pushed f959a986.
body.description: prim_mcp_send(tool_key, args_key) -> dispatch MCP tool call, store result in basket[out_key]. Synchronous in v1 (blocks step chain until result). prim_mcp_await(key, timeout_sec) -> poll basket[key] until set or timeout, write __error__ on timeout. Lets habits call Igor MCP tools (igor__cc_send, igor__memory_search, etc.) without code_ref boilerplate. Thin wrapper on existing tool registry dispatch.
